name: Eastern Andalusian Spanish
notes: Eastern Andalusian (Granada, Almería, Jaén). Maintains distinción (/θ/ vs /s/) but shares aspiration with west. Distinctive vowel opening when final -s is deleted, creating near-phonemic open/closed vowel pairs (approaching 10-vowel system): perros [ˈpɛrɔ] vs perro [ˈpero]. Affricate [tʃ] preserved (no deaffrication).

sources.author: Penny, R.
sources.title: A History of the Spanish Language

location.notes: Granada — a representative city of the eastern Andalusian area (with Almería and Jaén). [Wikidata Q8810 P625]

allophone_rules.notes: Eastern Andalusian vocalismo abierto: the mid/low vowel before an aspirated or lost /s/ opens, and the open vowel becomes the sole surviving cue to the plural / 2sg morpheme once the -s itself is gone (Navarro Tomas 1939, El acento andaluz / vocalismo; Cruz Ortiz, Sociofonetica andaluza). ⟨e⟩ before the [h] reflex of coda/final -s opens to [ɛ]: tres [ˈtɾɛ(h)], puestas [ˈpwɛhtæ]. Fires on the phoneme-level right-neighbour [h] (the only source of [h] in the eastern lect, since ⟨j⟩ keeps its velar [x] here).
⟨o⟩ before the [h] reflex of coda/final -s opens to [ɔ]: estos [ˈɛhtɔ], kilos [ˈkilɔ], euros [ˈɛwɾɔ] (Navarro Tomas 1939; Cruz Ortiz). See EAND_OPEN_E_BEFORE_S.

⟨a⟩ before the [h] reflex of coda/final -s opens to [æ]: tazas [ˈtaθæ], las [ˈlæ], buenas [ˈbwenæ] (Navarro Tomas 1939; Cruz Ortiz). High vowels /i u/ resist opening and are deliberately not rewritten (buscas [ˈbuhkæ], not [ˈbʊhkæ]).

After the preceding vowel has opened, a WORD-FINAL aspirated -s is dropped, so the open vowel alone marks the plural: las tres [læ ˈtɾɛ], los libros [lɔ ˈliβɾɔ]. A word-INTERNAL coda [h] (before a consonant) is kept: estos [ˈɛhtɔ] retains the first [h]. Ordered after the opening rules so the [h] has already conditioned the vowel before it is removed. RESIDUAL / honest scoping: true leftward VOWEL HARMONY across an intervening consonant (the euros e-opening, [ˈɛwɾɔ], where the stressed vowel is not itself adjacent to an -s) is NOT modelled — the allophone layer's neighbour conditions are segment-adjacent (immediate, or one grapheme away by class), so an autosegmental vowel-tier that skips consonants is not expressible here. Only LOCAL opening (a mid/low vowel immediately before the [h] of -s) is applied; this matches the arbitrated gold everywhere except the single euros-type token, which stays half-open.
